DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, JUL-08-2021 HEARING HELD RE: HEARINGS RE: DEFENDANT MISSION NEIGHBORHOOD CENTERS, INC.'S 1.) MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY; 2.) MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, AND FOR SANCTIONS, AND 3.) MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO DEFENDANTS REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND THINGS, AND FOR SANCTIONS. THE COURT RULES AS FOLLOWS: THE COURT FINDS THE MOTIONS WERE TIMELY FILED AND OVERRULES PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS AS TO PRELIMINARY ISSUES. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH LEAVES BLANK THE FUTURE HEARING DATE FOR THE FURTHER HEARING ON THIS MOTION. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR DETAILS.) JUDGE PRO TEM: STEVEN STEIN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

Off Calendar Nov-30-2021 Continued To Feb-23-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Hearing For Review Of Discovery Orders: July 8, 2021 Motions
Off Calendar Aug-26-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Hearing held on Wednesday, August 25, 2021 at 1:30PM via separate zoom link with JPT. Clerk not present.

Hearing For Review Of Discovery Orders: July 8, 2021 Motions

1.) MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY: After oral argument the Court takes the matter under submission. The moving party to forward to the Court by email the exhibits which are the subject of the Requests for Admissions.

2.) MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND FOR SANCTIONS: Motion granted in part and denied in part. All objections except as to overbroad and privilege are overruled. The Court modifies the categories of inquiry to resolve the sustained objection of overbroad. Plaintiff to respond to modified categories within twenty days of service of the signed Order. Defendant to prepare proposed Order and meet and confer with plaintiff to reach agreement on language of modified categories to resolve overbroad objection. If disagreement the parties shall submit their respective versions of proposed Order and the Court will rule. The Court maintains jurisdiction with respect to sufficiency of responses. Defendant's request for sanctions is denied.

3.) MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO DEFENDANTS REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND THINGS, AND FOR SANCTIONS: Motion granted in part and denied in part. All objections except as to overbroad and privilege are overruled. The Court modifies the categories of inquiry to resolve the sustained objection of overbroad. Plaintiff to respond to modified categories within twenty days of service of the signed Order. Defendant to prepare proposed Order and meet and confer with plaintiff to reach agreement on language of modified categories to resolve overbroad objection. If disagreement the parties shall submit their respective versions of proposed Order and the Court will rule. The Court maintains jurisdiction with respect to sufficiency of responses. Defendant's request for sanctions is denied.

Judge Pro Tem Steven Stein. (302)

Hearing For Review Of Discovery Orders: July 8, 2021 Motions

Hearing For Review Of Discovery Orders: July 8, 2021 Motions
Off Calendar Jul-26-2021 Continued To Aug-18-21 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 302

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendants Request For Production Of Documents And Things, And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-08-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. ARMAN MARUKYAN, Esq. of Lawyers for Justice, PC (Tel: 818-265-1020) for plaintiff;
Atty. LISA G. SCHLITTNER, Esq. of Cathy Arias Law (Tel: 925-412-3529) for defendant Mission Neighborhood Centers, Inc.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT MISSION NEIGHBORHOOD CENTERS, INC.'s Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendants Request For Production Of Documents And Things, And For Sanctions.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Judge Pro Tem and and counsel for both sides (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued. The Court rules as follows: The Court finds the motion was timely filed and overrules plaintiff's objections as to preliminary issues.

Plaintiff's counsel is to prepare and submit a proposed order to opposing counsel for review by 7/13/21. By 7/15/21, the parties are then to submit either an approved proposed order or competing proposed orders in Word format to the Judge Pro Tem at sbs@sbslawsf.com. The proposed order should recite the process by which defense counsel is to submit a Word format pleading of defendants' requests but should leave the deadline for this blank. The proposed order should also leave blank the future hearing date for the further hearing on this motion.

Judge Pro Tem: Steven Stein; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
